message:
chore(submodule): set ignore=dirty on legacy_sqlite_code/sqlite to silence inner working-tree drift

Add `ignore = dirty` to the `legacy_sqlite_code/sqlite` submodule
entry in `.gitmodules` so `git status` stops surfacing the vendored
sqlite tree every time something inside it gets touched (rebuild
artifacts, editor swap files, partial recovery dumps from the
legacy-format work).

Previously, any modification inside the submodule would make the
parent repo show

      m legacy_sqlite_code/sqlite

which added noise to every `git status`. The `m` (lowercase) means
"submodule has uncommitted modifications in its working tree" —
NOT that the submodule's recorded commit pointer changed. The
recorded commit pointer is what actually matters for the parent
repo, and that is still tracked normally by git submodule: any
intentional pointer update (e.g. bumping the vendored sqlite
version) will still show up as a normal modification in the
parent, because `ignore = dirty` only suppresses dirty-working-tree
reporting, not pointer changes.

This is the correct mechanism for a vendored read-only submodule
where the parent repo does not care about the inner working tree
state. It cannot be done via .gitignore (the parent's .gitignore
cannot reach into a submodule's tracked state) — .gitmodules is
the only place where the behavior can be configured per-submodule.

Added with a short explanatory comment above the `ignore` line so
future readers do not have to guess why the setting exists.

files.patch:
@@ -1,3 +1,7 @@
 [submodule "legacy_sqlite_code/sqlite"]
 	path = legacy_sqlite_code/sqlite
 	url = https://github.com/sqlite/sqlite
+	# Suppress "-dirty" status from uncommitted changes inside the vendored sqlite
+	# tree so our `git status` stays focused on our own code. The submodule commit
+	# itself is still tracked normally; only the inner working-tree drift is hidden.
+	ignore = dirty
